summary: DHH announces switching his blog from Greymatter to MovableType after Greymatter reset his database. He migrated recent entries and redesigned the site, attempting XHTML compliance but falling back to tables-based design due to IE 5.2/Mac rendering issues. The post is a brief, lighthearted update about his blogging infrastructure.
key_points: Switched from Greymatter to MovableType after a database reset incident destroyed trust in the platform
Attempted XHTML standards compliance but abandoned it due to IE 5.2/Mac rendering problems
Fell back to tables-based layout as a pragmatic compromise over standards purity
money_quotes: After the former decided to reset my database some time back, I've been weary about committing any more entries to the monster.

I even went as far as to shoot for XHTML standard compliance, but now that I'm in the Mac downstream, and couldn't get everything showing correctly on IE 5.2/Mac, I called it a day and tailed back to a tables design.
Forgive me, W3C father, for I have sinned. I will amend my ways as soon as time for tinkering permits it.
key_insight: Pragmatism beats standards purity — when browser compatibility breaks your ideals, ship what works and fix it later.
